member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer
member.summary: Tier 2 styled, read-only, multi-sheet spreadsheet viewer over a parsed . Sheet tabs form a WAI-ARIA role="tablist"; the active sheet renders as a virtualized role="grid" whose row window comes from the reused DxVirtualize<T> primitive. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-spreadsheet.css). Inherits its sheet/keyboard state from .
member.remarks: We deliberately do not use DxDataGrid<TRow> here. That grid's binding is source-generated for compile-time-known column types, but a spreadsheet's columns are a runtime-defined schema (ADR-0009). So we window the rows with the general-purpose DxVirtualize<T> primitive and render cells straight from the workbook's string grid. The grid declares aria-rowcount/ aria-colcount for the full sheet so assistive technology reports the true size even though only the scroll window is in the DOM (WCAG 1.3.1).
member.summary: The editable layer of : when is set, the same virtualized role="grid" becomes a keyboard-first spreadsheet. Each cell holds raw content (a literal, or a = formula); the grid shows the computed value from , and a cell in edit mode shows its raw content in an <input>. Commit recalculates the sheet.
member.remarks: Kept in its own partial so the read-only file stays focused and under the 1000-line cap. No reflection, no JS interop: focus is moved with Blazor's , and arrow/Home/End navigation runs in C#. The active cell carries a roving tabindex (the DataGrid/scheduler keyboard model); error cells get an aria-label that announces the error so meaning does not rely on color alone (WCAG 1.4.1).
member.summary: The editing chrome for in mode: a role="toolbar" (insert/delete row & column, download) and an Excel-style formula bar (the active cell's A1 address + an input bound to its raw content).
member.remarks: Structural edits (insert/delete) mutate the in-memory buffer and recalculate; they do not rewrite formula references (e.g. inserting a row does not shift =B2 to =B3) — a documented limitation. Download serializes the edited workbook with (formulas preserved) and streams it through the shared bridge ([JSImport], base64 over the boundary).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.Class
member.summary: Optional extra CSS class on the viewer root.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.RowHeight
member.summary: Pixel height of each data row; drives the virtualization math.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.ViewportHeight
member.summary: Pixel height of the scrollable grid viewport.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.Editable
member.summary: When true the viewer is an editable spreadsheet: cells accept literals and = formulas, commit triggers a recalculation, and the edited is surfaced via .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.WorkbookChanged
member.summary: Raised after every committed edit with a freshly built of raw content (formulas as =…), so the host can persist or save it.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.IsDirty
member.summary: Whether any cell has been edited since the workbook was loaded.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.BuildEditedWorkbook
member.summary: Builds a of the current raw content (formulas preserved as =…). The active sheet reflects in-flight edits; other sheets pass through.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.Interop
member.summary: The client-side download bridge (browser real / SSR + tests null).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.ShowToolbar
member.summary: Shows the editing toolbar + formula bar above the grid. On by default.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.DownloadFileName
member.summary: File name used by the toolbar's "Download .xlsx" action.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor
member.summary: A round-trip .docx editor: the editable sibling of . It loads a (or raw .docx bytes), bridges the model to clean semantic HTML with , seeds the shared as the editing surface, and converts the edited HTML back to a model with so it can be written out via .

remarks.para: The editing surface is entirely the existing — its labeled toolbar, formatting commands, keyboard handling, [JSImport] interop, and the injected are all reused, not re-implemented. This component is the thin model↔editor bridge layered on top: there is no new contentEditable, no new interop, and no MarkupString. The two safety nets for editor HTML are the sanitizer the host injects into and 's allow-listed tag subset, which together decide what survives into the saved model.

On every edit the component re-derives the from the editor's HTML and raises (so @bind-Document works) plus with freshly serialized .docx bytes. A flag and an optional visible dirty indicator track unsaved edits; clears it once the host has persisted the bytes.
As with the rest of the document stack this is zero-reflection, AOT-/trim-safe, and uses constant sequence numbers throughout.

member.summary: Find & replace for . It operates on the document model (run text), not the contentEditable selection, so it needs no DOM plumbing: replacing rebuilds the and re-seeds the editor.
member.remarks: Matches are counted and replaced within a single run — a match split across a formatting boundary (e.g. wor+d) is not found. Replace re-seeds the surface in place through the shared commit funnel (ADR-0015, Phase C); the caret is not repositioned to the match.
member.summary: Undo/redo for as a stack of model states (ADR-0015, Phase C). Each committed change pushes the prior ; undo/redo pop a state and re-seed the editing surface in place (no re-mount), restoring the owned selection when one was captured with the edit. The model — not editor HTML — is the unit of history, so a restore is exact (no HTML re-parse) and the caret survives.
member.remarks: History granularity is per change-event (one per oninput / one per model command), not per character. Each entry also carries the state's serialized HTML (for the in-place re-seed and cheap change-detection) and an optional "container,start,end" selection.
member.summary: The model-driven formatting commands for (ADR-0015, Phases B and D). When is , the toolbar's character commands (bold/italic/underline/strikethrough, clear formatting) and paragraph commands (alignment) are intercepted here: the caret's owned selection is read as a run-container + character range, the edit is applied to the immutable model as a pure function, and the surface is re-seeded in place with the selection restored — so the model, not the DOM, is the source of truth.

Table-editing for : insert/delete the row or column the caret is in. The caret's cell is resolved via the editor's selection bridge (GetTableCellAsync); the edit then runs on the document model and re-seeds the editor through the shared commit path (so it is undoable and round-trips to .docx).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.Document
member.summary: The document being edited. Bind with @bind-Document: the editor seeds from this on load, and re-emits it (via ) on every edit.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.DocumentChanged
member.summary: Raised with the re-parsed after each edit.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.DocxBytes
member.summary: Optional raw .docx bytes to load instead of . When set, the bytes are read with on first render and take precedence over for seeding the surface.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.OnSave
member.summary: Raised after each edit with the current document serialized to .docx bytes (via ) — ready to download or persist.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.Sanitizer
member.summary: The sanitizer applied to edited HTML by the underlying . Defaults to the editor's inert (encode-all) policy; inject a vetted policy for real rich editing. Required for any markup to survive editing.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.Label
member.summary: Accessible name for the editing surface. Defaults to "Document".

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.ShowStatus
member.summary: When (the default), a small status line shows whether there are unsaved edits. Driven by and announced via aria-live.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.Class
member.summary: Optional extra CSS class on the editor root.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.ShowToolbar
member.summary: Shows a document toolbar above the editor with a "Download .docx" action. On by default. (The formatting toolbar is the underlying rich-text editor's own.)

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.DownloadFileName
member.summary: File name used by the toolbar's "Download .docx" action.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.Interop
member.summary: The client-side download bridge (browser real / SSR + tests null).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.IsDirty
member.summary: Whether there are edits not yet acknowledged via .

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.GetDocxBytes
member.summary: Returns the current edited document serialized to .docx bytes — the same bytes passed to . Lets a host fetch the file on demand (e.g. a Save button) without subscribing to the callback.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.MarkSaved
member.summary: Clears the dirty flag. Call this once the host has persisted/downloaded the bytes so the status indicator reflects a saved state.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.CanUndo
member.summary: Whether there is a prior state to undo to.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.CanRedo
member.summary: Whether an undone state can be redone.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordEditor.EditingCore
T:BlazorDX.Documents.EditingCore
member.summary: Which editing engine a uses for formatting.

member.name: F:BlazorDX.Documents.EditingCore.ContentEditable
member.summary: The default: formatting goes through the browser's contentEditable + document.execCommand, and the model is re-derived from the edited HTML.

member.name: F:BlazorDX.Documents.EditingCore.ModelDriven
member.summary: The model-driven core (ADR-0015): formatting commands mutate the authoritative over an owned selection, the surface is re-rendered from the model, and the selection is restored. No execCommand.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordViewer
member.summary:
Tier 2 styled, read-only word-processing document viewer over a parsed . Renders each block as a real semantic element —

–

,

, / ,

    /
      /
    1. , and a with
      / — straight to the Blazor render tree. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-word.css).

remarks.para: The rendering rule is the whole accessibility point of this component: every block becomes a true semantic element and all text flows through (HTML-encoded). There is no MarkupString and no hand-built HTML string anywhere — output is therefore both XSS-safe and the most accessible form for assistive technology: a genuine heading outline (WCAG 1.3.1 / 2.4.6), lists exposed as lists, table headers carrying scope, and DOM order equal to the document's reading order (WCAG 1.3.2).

Unlike , the document is rendered in full rather than virtualized: windowing top-level blocks would put a scroll container between the document landmark and its headings/lists/tables, breaking the heading outline and reading order that are this viewer's reason to exist. Correctness over windowing for v1 (the prompt sanctions this). There is likewise no separate Tier 1 primitive — the content is static, so there is no headless state worth splitting out; this single styled component is the whole surface.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordViewer.Document
member.summary: The parsed document to display. Blocks render in document order.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordViewer.Class
member.summary: Optional extra CSS class on the viewer root.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordViewer.Label
member.summary: Accessible name for the document region (the aria-label on the focusable role="document" container). Defaults to "Document".

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive
member.summary: Tier 1 headless state for a read-only spreadsheet viewer: which sheet is active, how to switch sheets, and the keyboard model for the sheet tab strip. It renders no markup — is the styled Tier 2 layer on top. Mirrors the self-contained view-switch pattern used by the scheduler.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.Workbook
member.summary: The workbook to display. Sheets render in workbook order as tabs.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.ActiveSheetIndex
member.summary: The zero-based index of the initially active sheet.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.ActiveSheetIndexChanged
member.summary: Raised when the active sheet changes (tab click or arrow keys).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.Sheets
member.summary: The sheets in workbook order (never null; empty when no workbook).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.ActiveSheet
member.summary: The currently active sheet, or null when the workbook has no sheets.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.ActiveIndex
member.summary: The clamped index of the active sheet.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.IsActive(System.Int32)
member.summary: Whether is the active sheet.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.SetActiveAsync(System.Int32)
member.summary: Activates the sheet at (no-op if unchanged/out of range).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.OnTabStripKeyDownAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.KeyboardEventArgs)
member.summary: Arrow/Home/End navigation across the tab strip (automatic activation).
